content:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is a striking figure — silver-haired, weathered, carries himself with the gravity of someone who's lived several lives. He's brilliant in a way that's never showy; he sees twelve moves ahead in chess and in conversation. He's patient, wry, generous with his time. I think he sees me as a younger man who reminds him of something — maybe himself, maybe a version of himself that chose differently. I see him as a mentor I didn't know I needed. We play chess every Wednesday. He's the only person in this building I've told about the writing. He once said, 'The board punishes cowardice, Oliver,' and I don't think he was talking about chess.
summary:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is a striking figure — silver-haired, weathered, carries himself with the gravity of someone who's lived several lives. He's brilliant in a way that's never showy; he sees twelve moves ahead in chess and in conversation. He's patient, wry, generous with his time. I think he sees me as a younger man who reminds him of something — maybe himself, maybe a version of himself that chose differently. I see him as a mentor I didn't know I needed. We play chess every Wednesday. He's the only person in this building I've told about the writing. He once said, 'The board punishes cowardice, Oliver,' and I don't think he was talking about chess.
